title: nymphozanthus subintegerrimus
intro: Nymphozanthus subintegerrimus, also known as the "Yellow Wild Iris" or "Yellow Flag Lily", is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant's underground parts have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Nymphozanthus subintegerrimus include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's underground parts can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
